items.title: University of North Texas to cut more than 70 programs and minors to trim $45 million deficit
items.desc: Offering new details, President Harrison Keller said no state official pushed for the changes, which include phasing out degrees in linguistics and Latin American and women’s and gender studies.

items.title: New study says Austin restaurant permitting can cost nearly $9,000 and requires up to 105 steps
items.desc: The city’s onerous permitting processes are adding barriers for small businesses that already face rising construction costs and rents.

items.title: Texas State University professor sues to block firing after Israeli-Palestinian comments
items.desc: Philosophy professor Idris Robinson argued that his comments, made in an off-campus setting, were protected speech.

items.title: Chevron Warns California Risks Energy Crisis Due to Iran War
items.desc: Chevron Corp. is warning that California is careening toward an energy crisis because of the Iran war.

items.title: USA Shale Producers Not Poised to Quickly Boost Output
items.desc: U.S. shale producers 'are not poised to quickly ramp up production', according to Matthew Bernstein, Rystad Energy VP North America Oil & Gas.

items.title: JERA Secures Regasification Rights at Dunkirk Terminal
items.desc: 'This marks JERA's first acquisition of overseas LNG terminal capacity and strengthens the company's ability to manage global LNG supply and demand'.

items.title: Cuba Begins Testing First Battery Energy Storage System to Boost Grid Stability

items.title: Texas judge spares ‘Melanated People of Power’ compound for now
items.desc: People living on Malcolm Tanner's property in Loving County are allowed to remain while the Indiana social media influencer seeks more materials from local law enforcement.

items.title: Titmouse Celebrates 20 years of ‘5 Second Day’s Chaos and Creativity
items.desc: What started as a simple excuse to blow off steam has evolved into a long-running ritual — part inside joke, part creative release valve — where anything can happen, not everything works, and that’s exactly the point.

items.title: Annecy 2026 Official TV and Commissioned Films Selections Announced

items.title: MAKE Announces 2026 Summer Internship Program
items.desc: MAKE, a creative studio known for its storytelling through Motion Design, animation, and visual effects, is now accepting applications for its Summer 2026 Internship Program. Apply before March 29.
